126014.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that in a village community like Voorschoten, the municipal council should be so approachable that residents feel directly heard. When a topic is very relevant and well-defined, a referendum can be a way to reach a decision.

126020.explanation: Currently there is no asylum reception in Voorschoten, but there is accommodation for status holders. GroenLinks-PvdA considers it only normal that Voorschoten takes responsibility to help people in need.

126023.explanation: On days with little wind or fog, smoke from wood stoves and fire pits often lingers in the air. This causes dirty air, irritation to eyes and lungs, and is harmful to the climate. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA wants to introduce a burning ban when the Burning Guide indicates it is necessary. But when conditions allow, fireplaces and wood stoves may be used.

126026.explanation: Collecting waste costs money and it is important that people are aware of this. GroenLinks-PvdA wants dropping off bulky waste to remain free but considers it logical that people pay when they have it collected.

126028.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities, but many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example to someone in a wheelchair. If entrepreneurs need it, GroenLinks-PvdA believes the municipality should assist with loans.

126029.explanation: There is a huge housing shortage and, for example, an extra caregiver’s house in the yard can be a good solution. However, GroenLinks-PvdA does not want to simply allow multiple houses on agricultural land, such as farmer Rodenburg’s land.

126030.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And they keep you healthy. But for people receiving social assistance, sports are often too expensive. GroenLinks-PvdA wants the municipality to ensure this group can join a sports club for free by introducing a Voorschoten Pass.

126031.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what’s happening in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to stay afloat. GroenLinks-PvdA believes the municipality should contribute to this.

126033.explanation: Children are under enormous pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. GroenLinks-PvdA wants music lessons to be accessible not only for children with wealthy parents but also, for example, through the Voorschoten Pass.

126034.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy and clubs are often meeting places. If a sports club shuts down, all of Voorschoten suffers. Through sports facility policies, GroenLinks-PvdA wants to contribute to this in a planned way.

126036.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA feels there is too little enforcement in our municipality. Voorschoten needs more BOAs (enforcement officers) who focus on safety and other important supervision tasks within the municipality.

126037.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But we do not think more cameras are the right solution. Cameras are expensive and require a lot of police capacity, which is lacking. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA opts for more enforcement officers on the street who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

126039.explanation: The problem for starters in Voorschoten is not lack of financing, but that homes have become utterly unaffordable. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to address that problem instead of offering starter loans that no one can use and that drive prices up even more.

126043.explanation: The rural area around Voorschoten is scarce natural land in a densely populated environment. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to make better use of land within our village first and prefers redevelopment.

126044.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes most people are not on social assistance because they do not want to work. They need a stable base and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions cause insecurity and create new problems.

126046.explanation: There is also a substantial group in our village who may just be above the minimum subsistence level but still have to figure out every month how to make ends meet. GroenLinks-PvdA does not want to abandon these people and wants to give them relief as well.

126048.explanation: There are still neighborhoods where charging points are scarce, making it difficult for people to choose an electric car. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA wants to invest in filling those gaps.

126049.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to adopt 30 km/h as the norm. This makes traffic in Voorschoten safer, especially for vulnerable groups like children and the elderly. It also improves air quality and reduces traffic noise nuisance.

126050.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that people must always be able to trust that they are well helped with respect for their faith or culture, no matter who helps them. This mutual respect goes both ways: caregivers also deserve respect for what they do and who they are.

126052.explanation: The property tax (OZB) for residences in Voorschoten is high, but for non-residential properties it is very low. At the same time, public amenities are under great pressure due to budget cuts from the national government. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to look at a fair distribution of the financial burden, but will not ruin Voorschoten by cutting too much.

126055.explanation: The business parks in Voorschoten are largely poorly utilized, with much space standing empty or being used for purposes they are not intended for. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to first clean this up and invest in the existing space.

126057.explanation: Voorschoten must help people who urgently need housing. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to apply the urgency scheme both for status holders and for people who get into difficulties due to illness, disability, caregiving, debts through no fault of their own, divorce with children, or demolition of their home.

126059.explanation: Solar panels can not only produce clean energy and be a good source of income for farmers, but in principle they can also be well combined with increased biodiversity. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to create space for the latter.

126060.explanation: The current facilities of Voorschoten'97 are in poor condition. GroenLinks-PvdA wants, as part of the sports accommodation policy, to look together with them and other clubs at the investments needed to keep the facilities and the clubs using them vital.

126062.explanation: According to GroenLinks-PvdA, there is currently no need for a coffeeshop in our municipality, also because there is sufficient availability in Leiden.

126063.explanation: There must be more affordable housing in our village. GroenLinks-PvdA wants at least 30% social housing in all new construction and finds that 65% of all delivered homes should be affordable. This also means more affordable housing outside the social segment.

126066.explanation: Although not all neighborhoods are large enough to have their own full neighborhood center, GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important to ensure that all neighborhoods have a nearby neighborhood center where people can go.

126067.explanation: The center of Voorschoten already has a lot of parking spaces with two parking garages and several parking lots. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to invest in an attractive center by reducing the number of cars in certain places.

126068.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that the center of Voorschoten – especially the Voorstraat – should primarily be a meeting place where people enjoy spending their time. Making the center car-free or car-reduced is an important step to free up space for our residents.

126070.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA envisions the Treubplein first and foremost as a centrally located place where people like to come for an ice cream, coffee, or shopping. This has priority over parking spaces that are already amply available in the area.

126014.explanation: Every four years, residents can express their opinions through elections. Legally, there is already sufficient input established for various topics.

126020.explanation: Everyone wants to solve problems as quickly as possible, but this is nationally imposed policy over which we have no local influence. The VVD cares about genuine refugees and believes humane shelter is necessary. However, in case of disturbances, quick and adequate intervention is required.

126023.explanation: The VVD stands for freedom of action; lighting fires must not cause nuisance and problems should basically be resolved among residents themselves.

126026.explanation: For many residents, the recycling center in Wassenaar is an obstacle to disposing of bulky waste. Free collection is a service the municipality should provide. The costs can be covered through municipal taxes.

126028.explanation: The VVD stands for freedom and entrepreneurship. Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for making their buildings accessible to people with disabilities or taking measures so they can serve this group. For public buildings, the municipality is obliged to ensure accessibility.

126029.explanation: More houses need to be built, but according to demand. The municipality should provide guidance based on the Spatial Vision (Omgevingsvisie).

126030.explanation: Sports are important, but there are already many subsidies from which club fees for sports associations can be paid.

126031.explanation: For the VVD, enough money already goes to the local broadcasters.

126033.explanation: Music lessons are important for a child’s development but are not a basic need. For children whose parents cannot afford it, tailored support should be possible.

126034.explanation: Healthy sports clubs are important to promote exercise for everyone. However, the municipality can only support if the sports club itself has done everything to resolve problems.

126036.explanation: Safety is a key priority for the VVD. Recently, the deployment of Enforcement Officers (BOA’s) has improved, but more BOA’s help further increase the feeling of safety for residents.

126037.explanation: Safety is a key priority for the VVD. Cameras at risk locations like stations help improve the feeling of safety. The VVD also advocates for ANPR cameras at the village’s exit roads.

126039.explanation: This concerns a loan that must be repaid. The loan may only be granted to residents already registered in Voorschoten.

126043.explanation: More houses need to be built. If necessary, rural areas should be sacrificed for this.

126044.explanation: Socially meaningful work is good for the self-esteem of people in difficult social positions and serves as a stepping stone to paid work, enabling these people to fully contribute to society again.

126046.explanation: The VVD believes that work must pay. People who need help should receive it through tailored solutions. A fixed income threshold makes tailored assistance more difficult. The VVD believes self-reliance helps people have a better place in society.

126048.explanation: At present, there are sufficient public charging stations in the village. If demand for charging stations increases, they should be added accordingly.

126049.explanation: In residential neighborhoods, speeds should be reduced back to 30 km/h. On main roads, the 50 km/h speed limit should be maintained to ensure smooth traffic flow.

126050.explanation: The VVD stands for freedom of choice, but if care is paid for by the government, it must be efficient and choice may be limited.

126052.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is already high in Voorschoten and should be lowered where possible or at least not raised.

126055.explanation: Only for the Dobbewijk. Expansion of the Dobbewijk is necessary to give our entrepreneurs room to grow. It also provides the opportunity to relocate small fragmented business parks to the Dobbewijk, thereby freeing up space for housing.

126057.explanation: The VVD understands that refugees with residence permits also need housing. But the need among our own residents is high, so first help Voorschoten residents find housing, then other groups. And build more so everyone can be accommodated.

126059.explanation: Promotes sustainability and creates capacity on the electricity grid, but risks must be carefully monitored.

126060.explanation: The VVD supports the recently adopted sports accommodation policy whereby sports clubs must pay for their own clubhouses and changing rooms. No exceptions should be made for any specific club.

126062.explanation: For people who want to use coffeeshop services, there are sufficient options in Leiden and The Hague.

126063.explanation: Housing in Voorschoten should be built according to demand. This means for the percentage of social rental housing: more than 28% if needed, less than 28% if possible.

126066.explanation: There are already sufficient community centers in Voorschoten.

126067.explanation: People like to come to the village by car and must be able to park. Good accessibility is vital for the village’s entrepreneurs.

126068.explanation: Schoolstraat is already car-free; Voorstraat can be made car-reduced but must remain accessible for residents and entrepreneurs.

126070.explanation: The current plan already provides for this.

126014.explanation: A referendum is good for the involvement of residents and securing broad support is very important.

126020.explanation: There is no reason to close asylum reception centers. In our municipality, there is no nuisance from asylum reception. It brings entrepreneurs a lot of extra income. As a municipality and community, we must not bow to blatant racism, but cherish our welcoming culture.

126023.explanation: Of course, fireplaces and stoves that harm health or the environment must be banned. There is no reason to prohibit wood stoves that comply with health and environmental standards.

126026.explanation: Free collection of bulky waste is a kind gesture to residents. That should be possible within the waste collection budget.

126028.explanation: It is in the interest of entrepreneurs to be accessible to a broad public. The costs are part of entrepreneurship.

126029.explanation: Provided it is properly regulated in the zoning plan.

126030.explanation: We believe that participating in sports can promote both social participation and health benefits. When people cannot participate in sports due to limited financial means, the exclusion of these people is reinforced.

126031.explanation: The local broadcaster is already partially funded by the government. The municipality can stimulate the local broadcaster by purchasing advertising on it.

126033.explanation: Through its subsidies, the municipality must enable children to attend one of the many music associations for free. It would be even better if regular music lessons were offered again at school for all children.

126034.explanation: This is too general. When sports clubs are struggling, it should be assessed what social contribution the club makes. That contribution should be eligible for subsidies.

126036.explanation: A commonly heard complaint is that enforcement does not happen. That is not entirely true, but there is certainly room for improvement.

126037.explanation: More camera surveillance in deserted places is definitely needed. Especially around parking lots and at public transport stations and stops.

126039.explanation: This already exists in the form of a municipal guarantee, but it is far from sufficient to make a difference. The financing gap for starters with an average income is far too large. A better solution is to quickly build affordable, low-cost starter homes.

126043.explanation: Preserving a green zone around the village is a great asset. There is no need to expand outside the built-up area as long as there are still enough unused spots within it.

126044.explanation: You might want and wish for that, both for the community and the individuals involved. However, without knowing the reason for the welfare benefit, no meaningful statement can be made.

126046.explanation: Poverty is not an absolute figure. It must be considered on a case-by-case basis.

126048.explanation: The number of charging points should slightly exceed the number of electric cars. Moreover, charging points should not become hangout spots for people overstaying at charging stations.

126049.explanation: Yes, 30 km/h should be the standard speed limit on all roads, except for the (two) provincial roads and roads used by public transport buses.

126050.explanation: That depends on the type of care.

126052.explanation: Property tax (OZB) should be used for the municipality's own policy choices. The municipality must be able to explain this well to residents. So far, the municipality cannot do that.

126055.explanation: There is no reason for that. Vacancy is actually increasing.

126057.explanation: Like other emergency seekers, such as people displaced by fire, domestic violence, or divorce.

126059.explanation: Farmland is for agriculture (or livestock). For 20 years, Voorschoten has invested a lot of money and energy in removing greenhouse constructions. This was intended to preserve the landscape values, especially in the Duivenvoorde corridor. We are not going to replace them with solar panels now.

126060.explanation: It must be well maintained, with occasional renewal of certain elements. A complete renovation is not necessary. This applies to all sports facilities, not just Voorschoten '97.

126062.explanation: If an entrepreneur sees a business opportunity there, that should be possible. Of course, connections with criminal organizations must be examined.

126063.explanation: This must, of course, meet the legal minimum of two-thirds affordable housing, of which at least 30% is social housing. We also believe that where possible, 100% affordable housing should be aimed for on municipal land.

126067.explanation: There are sufficient parking spaces. Only if additional spaces can be built underground without taking up surface space should more parking places be added.

126068.explanation: Provided that delivery and emergency services can maintain good access.

126070.explanation: In the short term, Treubplein can be revamped without removing most parking spaces. The concerns of entrepreneurs are justified. If in the future, say in 5 to 10 years, there is less need for parking spaces, the next step can be taken.

126014.explanation: Referendums reduce complex decisions to a yes or no choice, which does not do justice to the complexity of many proposals. Referendums increase and sharpen divisions. Politicians must actively engage with people. We prefer that our politicians talk with people, not about them.

126020.explanation: We make agreements in the region about asylum reception and housing. This way, with our neighboring municipalities, we can use the possibilities for spreading target groups and mutual exchange of these groups to be housed. This allows us to ensure asylum reception appropriate to our community.

126023.explanation: There is a burning guide in the Netherlands. When it shows orange or red, the advice is not to burn. Using a fireplace can cause nuisance. We believe the municipality should remind residents of their responsibility.

126026.explanation: The amount of residual waste must be greatly reduced. Additional price or service incentives (including partly variable rates) are not taboo for us, as long as residents receive sufficient support to separate their waste.

126028.explanation: The municipality can help entrepreneurs make their buildings more accessible for people with disabilities. This can be through a subsidy, low-interest loan, or a fiscal arrangement. The CDA wants an accessible society for everyone.

126029.explanation: If farmers want to sell their land on their own initiative and develop housing, we should not exclude that. If, with everyone's support, we can ensure more homes are built, we support that. It is crucial that the farmer wants this themselves.

126030.explanation: Basic services remain accessible to everyone. We simplify the rules, guide and support people. No complicated forms, but real help for our low-income residents. We want people to be better helped and customized solutions to be possible.

126031.explanation: Local newspaper, local radio, and local television are important to us. A local broadcaster is a gem for our village. It also showcases our events. And not least, they monitor what happens in the town hall. We must cherish and support this.

126033.explanation: Music education is important for children. We want it to be easily accessible to children. We want this through strong music associations that help keep contributions as low as possible. For families who find it harder, we provide extra support through, for example, Stichting Leergeld.

126034.explanation: The CDA has successfully worked in the past period on establishing the sports facility policy. This policy focuses on accessible and affordable sports facilities so that sports are available to all ages and backgrounds. Associations can rely on this policy.

126036.explanation: We advocate for a full police station in Voorschoten, where neighborhood officers can work well together with the BOAs. We welcome the potential arrival of a police station. We want to invest in the BOAs.

126037.explanation: A safe society goes beyond deploying enforcement and police. The community itself also plays a major role in ensuring (social) safety. A great example of looking out for one another. If crime persists, we do not rule out camera surveillance at the station.

126039.explanation: Starters have a very hard time in this housing market. In new developments, we focus on smaller affordable owner-occupied and rental homes for seniors, starters, youth, and those in need of assistance. This creates the desired housing flow. CDA wants to help starters in this way.

126043.explanation: Our green space in Voorschoten is limited. That means our basic stance is to keep green spaces green in Voorschoten. At the same time, we tackle the housing shortage, which for us means preferably building inward, adding extra floors, and redevelopment.

126044.explanation: The government is the shield of the vulnerable. At the same time, we ensure that eventually everyone can stand on their own feet. A job is the best guarantee for dignity and participation in society. If that is not possible, help is provided for meaningful day activities. We do not let anyone fall through the cracks.

126046.explanation: The CDA wants people who need it to receive the help they require. The government is the shield of the vulnerable. We do not let anyone fall through the cracks. Especially for those on minimum incomes, Voorschoten must be accessible, strong, and social. Income security is necessary for healthy living and growing up.

126048.explanation: There are increasingly more electric cars in Voorschoten. That means more charging points are needed. We will expand these charging points but not at the expense of regular parking spaces.

126049.explanation: We make residential neighborhoods more family-friendly and implement 30 km/h zones where possible. Every study shows: 30 km/h results in fewer accidents. We also want safer crossing places and other safety-enhancing measures, such as separating car and bicycle traffic.

126050.explanation: This is not an obligation but a preference. So many backgrounds, so many wishes. We want people to have the choice to express their preference. At the same time, care is a scarce resource. There are too few hands available. So it may happen that you get a different care provider.

126052.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is already high compared to other municipalities. Compared to the region, Voorschoten has high resident charges. We want to keep a close eye on the balance between the OZB for non-residential properties and the OZB for residential properties.

126055.explanation: We actively enforce against vacancy, undesirable use, and fragmentation of shopping areas and business parks. We want sufficient space in our business parks for manufacturing and contractor companies. This must not come at the expense of green spaces in Voorschoten.

126057.explanation: There is a large shortage of social rental housing. The CDA wants to distribute these fairly among everyone. A portion should be available for status holders, but this must not disproportionately come at the expense of other home seekers who often have been waiting a long time for a home.

126059.explanation: First collect solar energy on roofs; we must be careful with good agricultural land. Solar panels belong primarily on roofs or above parking lots. This way, we preserve our open green spaces.

126060.explanation: The municipality can help Voorschoten by providing guarantees for renewing their sports accommodations. CDA stands firmly for healthy sports clubs.

126062.explanation: The CDA is opposed to a coffeeshop in Voorschoten.

126063.explanation: The backlog in (new) construction of social housing must be caught up. More social rental homes must be delivered as a priority. We are joining forces with the housing corporations Woonzorg, De Sleutels, and Rijnhart.

126066.explanation: We preserve community centers and all other facilities that make a neighborhood lively. We find it important that sufficient facilities are available for residents in both existing and new neighborhoods. By investing in the 'public capital,' we strengthen livability.

126067.explanation: We ensure sufficient parking facilities and a good balance between car traffic, cyclists, and pedestrians. At Treubplein, we provide enough permanent parking places balanced with the space needed for shopkeepers and the redevelopment of the square itself.

126068.explanation: Easily accessible shops are crucial for entrepreneurs. The CDA therefore wants to preserve parking spaces nearby. This is also important for our senior residents.

126070.explanation: All entrepreneurs share the opinion that Treubplein deserves a facelift. At the same time, as many parking spaces as possible should be preserved. The redesign can only take place with support from the center entrepreneurs. Treubplein must become as green as possible with sufficient parking spaces.

126014.explanation: D66 wants good participation from residents and transparency about decisions.

126020.explanation: D66 adheres to the distribution law.

126023.explanation: D66 wants attention for the nuisance from smoke and air quality so that people adjust their burning behavior to the circumstances.

126026.explanation: D66 wants waste to be collected separately, which is not possible with one garbage truck. Otherwise, everything ends up in one garbage truck.

126028.explanation: D66 wants buildings to be accessible to everyone and, if necessary, is willing to contribute to that.

126029.explanation: D66 wants more housing, fitting within regulations.

126030.explanation: D66 believes that sports and exercise should be accessible to everyone.

126031.explanation: D66 considers the role of an independent press to be of great importance.

126033.explanation: D66 thinks that besides arithmetic and language, music and other creative subjects add a lot of value.

126034.explanation: D66 considers sport and exercise an important foundation for a healthy and vital life. Temporary support for sports clubs in difficulties is possible.

126036.explanation: D66 wants police and BOAs to work well together to ensure good enforcement.

126037.explanation: D66 wants camera surveillance only (temporarily) if there is a clear and urgent need.

126039.explanation: D66 does not see this as a solution for starters. The issue is having enough homes.

126043.explanation: D66 prefers building within the built-up area.

126044.explanation: D66 finds it important that people participate according to their abilities, which is not possible for everyone.

126046.explanation: D66 opposes fragmentation of policy so that residents clearly know what they are entitled to.

126048.explanation: D66 believes that when demand increases, supply must keep pace.

126049.explanation: D66 wants an exception only for main roads.

126050.explanation: D66 wants people to have choices. That does not mean their preference will always be realized.

126052.explanation: D66 wants to limit the increase but not at the expense of facilities. A realistic budget is of great importance.

126055.explanation: D66 wants to support economic growth, as long as it fits within the environment.

126057.explanation: D66 wants everyone to have a place. Some of the homes can be used for refugees. The real solutions are building more homes and improving existing ones.

126059.explanation: D66 wants solar panels to initially be placed on the roofs of homes, businesses, and schools.

126060.explanation: D66 supports all sports clubs with the sports facilities policy and wants the availability, use, and maintenance of sports facilities to also be considered on a regional level.

126062.explanation: D66 is not opposed by definition. Enforcement to prevent nuisance is very important in this regard.

126063.explanation: D66 wants more social rental homes to be used to catch up on the backlog.

126066.explanation: For D66, community centers are important places for meeting, cohesion, and prevention. The community center has an important function in a neighborhood.

126067.explanation: D66 believes there are sufficient parking spaces.

126068.explanation: D66 wants to make part of the center car-free, such as the Voorstraat. We take into account residents and people with limited mobility.

126070.explanation: D66 believes there must be parking availability in the center. The accessibility of the center is important. There are hundreds of parking spaces, spread across parking garages (Deltaplein, AH and Hema), parking lots such as at Churchillplein and Ambachtspad, and street parking within walking distance.

126014.explanation: It increasingly happens that Voorschoten residents feel unheard about a decision. With a referendum, Voorschoten takes a new step in democratic decision-making.

126020.explanation: Asylum seekers also need a roof over their heads. You don't solve any problem by letting people fleeing war and violence sleep on the streets. The current shelter does not cause problems. There is no reason for closure.

126023.explanation: A wood fire is cozy, but no longer up to date. Wood burning promotes asthma. For public health reasons, we want a transitional period first and then a ban.

126026.explanation: The SP wants offering waste to be easy. But free collection means an increase in the waste disposal charge, because the costs for the municipality rise.

126028.explanation: Improving accessibility is important. Entrepreneurs are legally obliged to arrange this in their own building. In special cases, the municipality can support. This does not have to be financial.

126029.explanation: The few agricultural lands in Voorschoten are outside the built-up area. We do not want uncontrolled building there, but as much open space as possible.

126030.explanation: Sports are important for everyone. We want to prevent people from being excluded because they cannot afford club membership fees. Therefore, we advocate introducing a Voorschoten pass. This makes it easy to arrange this.

126031.explanation: Local media are very important. We believe there should be sufficient resources for local reporting.

126033.explanation: Culture and learning to play an instrument are important for everyone. We want to prevent children from being excluded because their parents cannot afford music lessons.

126034.explanation: We certainly want to help small clubs in trouble, but we do not guarantee (semi-)professional adventures.

126036.explanation: The SP especially wants the BOAs to be more visible. They should walk more on the streets and cycle more in the neighborhoods. This makes them more approachable for residents.

126037.explanation: CCTV has an important downside. Footage can be misused. It is alarming how people can already be tracked from control rooms.

126039.explanation: House prices in Voorschoten are skyrocketing. The average sale price is now almost 600,000 euros. Young starters cannot afford this. With a starter loan, the municipality can help to buy that first home.

126043.explanation: The little greenery at the edges of Voorschoten must remain green. We build houses in the village.

126044.explanation: The SP supports a social assistance scheme based on trust. People with benefits can get support to return to work if they are able. But this must never be a condition for receiving benefits.

126046.explanation: More and more people live in poverty, even those with jobs. These people must also be able to use the municipality’s poverty schemes. Therefore, the income limit must be raised to 130% of the social minimum for all schemes.

126048.explanation: Electric driving is the future. We must prepare for it now.

126049.explanation: The SP wants a maximum speed of 30 km/h on all roads, except on main roads to Leiden, Wassenaar, and Leidschendam.

126050.explanation: The SP supports a diverse range of care providers. But quality is more important. Only care providers who can offer the required quality may be contracted.

126052.explanation: The SP voted for this in the municipal council in 2025. The property tax (OZB) for residents in Voorschoten is quite high. In the long term, we want to make the OZB fairer. Companies pay relatively very little now. We also do not mind if people with very expensive houses contribute more to the village.

126055.explanation: Space on industrial estates can be better managed, especially in Dobbewijk. The SP wants redesign, not expansion.

126057.explanation: This is arranged regionally: percentage of available homes with Holland Rijnland. And it should stay that way. These people must move out of the asylum seekers’ center and build a life in the Netherlands. We do not want them to end up on the street. We therefore treat status holders the same as other urgent cases.

126059.explanation: There are no suitable places in Voorschoten for this. The SP would like to place solar panels on the railway embankment.

126060.explanation: Voorschoten ’97 has just received a new athletics track. The SP lobbied for this for years. Renovation of sports hall De Vliethorst now has priority. Renovation of Voorschoten ’97’s facilities will be included in multi-year maintenance plans.

126062.explanation: We have nothing against a coffeeshop, but we wonder if Voorschoten is the right place.

126063.explanation: Voorschoten has far less than 28% social rental housing. We must make up our backlog and build much more than 28% social rental. This is the absolute minimum.

126066.explanation: Neighborhood centers ensure cohesion in the neighborhood.

126067.explanation: There are parking spaces available in the center. The Deltaplein parking garage always has enough space.

126068.explanation: The SP wants the Voorstraat to become low-traffic. This means only residents and loading/unloading are allowed.

126070.explanation: The SP wants terraces, for example from the ice cream shop, at Treubplein. Driving cars make that less attractive. Parking spaces can be located just next to Treubplein, so parking is also possible near the square.

statements.title: When redeveloping Treubplein, at least 60% of the current parking spaces must be retained.

titleParts.glossary: The redesign or alteration of a street or square. For example: different paving, new plants and trees, or traffic measures like speed bumps.

titleParts.text: van het Treubplein moet ten minste 60% van de huidige parkeerplaatsen behouden blijven.

statements.title: At least 28% of the newly built homes in Voorschoten must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Voorschoten moet minimaal 28% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: In Voorschoten the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Voorschoten moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The residents of Voorschoten must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Voorschoten moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Voorschoten must have increased camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: In Voorschoten the business parks may expand.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Voorschoten must renovate the Voorschoten97 facility.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Voorschoten must deploy more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The center of Voorschoten must become car-free.
